pages.full_text:
July 25 1990
Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, Peter
off to the Ardèche, France, & to
visit the Wegners in W. Germany.
	John & Charles looked after by
Nanny Helen, Jim, Margaret, Helen,
Rosemary, David & John Young
	We set off via Rugby, to take
phtos to Decorum. Lovely hot
sunny weather. We had lunch near
Rugby & Crick, on farmland. Simon
had been mending the hot tap &
forgotten to put the tap back on.
(We have now solved that by using
the on/off switch.) At 4.30. we
went through the Dartford Tunnel
with no delays. Half an hour later
we heard on the radio that there were
1½ mile queues in either direction
	Wednesday night we spent camped
pages.notes: Handwritten page number at top-left is not clearly legible — the very top of the image is slightly cropped. The number '3936' visible at top-right of the left image likely spans the gutter and refers to the right page. 'phtos' is written as-is (likely 'photos'). 'Decorum' is an uncertain reading — could be a place name or abbreviation; possibly 'Decorum' is a misread of a destination like 'Decorating' or it may be a proper noun unfamiliar to transcriber. 'Ardèche' is spelled with accent as written. The Wegners are a family in West Germany. The entry continues on the right page.

pages.full_text:
on the road outside the Edwards'
house. Lee, Richard, Andrew 11,
Michael 9. It was [?Andrew's] Michael's last day
ever at primary school & he
went off to a party. Thomas,
William, Peter & Andrew played
football on the lawn & lego.
We had a delicious turkey supper
& tart. __Canterbury__, the day the new archbishop cho[se]
__Thursday__ We set off at 8.50.
& came across on the Pride of
Calais, big & clean. We had lunch
outside a farm barn near St. Omer,
then went on a motorway A26.
			Arras
				coffee &
				orangina in
				the square
pages.notes: The right page number at top-right appears to be '38' (partially visible). There is a hand-drawn sketch of a building (appears to be a decorative illustration of a European-style building with a bell tower or belfry) in the lower-left area of the page, labelled 'Arras' above it with 'coffee & orangina in the square' to the right — this is a decorative sketch of a building in Arras, not a route map. The word 'Thursday' is underlined. 'Canterbury' is underlined. The sentence '... Canterbury, the day the new archbishop cho[se]' is cut off at the right edge of the page — the word after 'cho' is not visible. The annotation above 'Andrew's' reads 'Michael's' written in superscript as a correction. The ferry 'Pride of Calais' operated the Dover–Calais route. The iso_guess for Thursday is tentative as the diary only gives 'Thursday' and the trip started 25 July 1990 (Wednesday), making Thursday the 26th.
